commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a maintainer security/upgrade choice: repair legacy binding enforcement or explicitly accept fresh-binding-only behavior.; Needs attention: The patch is security-boundary relevant and still leaves existing binding records without agentId on the older fallback policy path.; Review finding: [P1] Handle bindings that lack agentId — extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.ts:246 (sha=95a7c4c5661873124fa0a31c33986f63a8298f97)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds an optional `agentId` to Codex app-server conversation binding data, passes it from `/codex bind`, and supplies it to the native execution guard with focused tests.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +55. Total +71 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: use an existing Codex app-server binding without `data.agentId`, no inbound session key, and a non-default bound agent configured with `tools.exec.host=node`; the guard evaluates fallback/default policy instead.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a maintainer security/upgrade choice: repair legacy binding enforcement or explicitly accept fresh-binding-only behavior.

commands.author: jesse-merhi

target.title: fix(codex): honor bound agent exec host policy
target.body:
## Summary
- store the binding agent id for newly created Codex app-server conversation bindings
- pass the stored binding agent id into the native execution guard
- carry the same binding agent id into app-server runtime policy resolution for bind, bound-turn, and missing-thread recovery paths
- prevent unrelated current-session exec-host overrides from masking a different bound agent's `tools.exec.host=node` policy
- preserve persisted unscoped/default-session exec-host and approval overrides when the explicit agent is the configured default agent

## What Changed
- `/codex bind` now passes the current session agent id into `startCodexConversationThread`.
- Codex app-server conversation binding data now persists optional `agentId` next to `sessionFile`, `workspaceDir`, and `agentDir`.
- Bound app-server inbound turns now pass `data.agentId` to the native execution guard, so agent-scoped `tools.exec.host=node` policy is checked even when the inbound event has no session key.
- App-server thread attach/create, later bound turns, and missing-thread recovery now pass the same bound `agentId` into runtime policy resolution, so approval and sandbox policy also come from the bound agent.
- Native execution policy ignores runtime session exec-host overrides when an explicit bound agent id points at a different agent than the inbound session key.
- Native execution policy still reads unscoped legacy/default session keys, such as `main` or `node-session`, when the explicit agent is the configured default agent, so persisted per-session `execHost` overrides are preserved.
- Bound-turn runtime policy ignores `execSecurity`/`execAsk` overrides from a different agent's session key, including shared session-store configurations.
- Command, conversation-binding, and native-execution-policy tests cover the new argument, no-session bound-turn blocks, agent-scoped app-server approval policy, missing-thread recovery, current-session override precedence, default-agent unscoped override preservation, and different-agent override isolation.

## Scope
This intentionally applies to newly created Codex app-server conversation bindings. Existing saved bindings that predate `data.agentId` can keep the previous fallback behavior until the conversation is detached and rebound.

That keeps this PR narrow: it does not add migration or legacy binding inference logic.

## Flow
```mermaid
flowchart LR
  subgraph before["Before this PR"]
    direction TB
    b1["Discord runs /codex bind"] --> b2["Binding stores sessionFile, workspaceDir, agentDir"]
    b2 --> b3["Bound agent id is not stored, example: discord-bot-a"]
    b3 --> b4["Later Discord message resumes the bound app-server session"]
    b4 --> b5["Inbound event may have no session key, or a session key for main"]
    b5 --> b6["Guard cannot reliably check discord-bot-a"]
    b6 --> b7["Runtime policy also lacks discord-bot-a"]
    b7 --> b8["Policy may fall back to default agent or current-session overrides"]
    b8 --> b9["App-server native tools may use the wrong exec, approval, or sandbox policy"]
  end

  subgraph after["After this PR, for new bindings"]
    direction TB
    a1["Discord runs /codex bind"] --> a2["Binding stores sessionFile, workspaceDir, agentDir, agentId"]
    a2 --> a3["Stored agent id example: discord-bot-a"]
    a3 --> a4["Later Discord message resumes the bound app-server session"]
    a4 --> a5["Inbound event has no session key, or a session key for main"]
    a5 --> a6["Guard checks discord-bot-a"]
    a6 --> a7{"discord-bot-a tools.exec.host"}
    a7 -- "node" --> a8["Block native app-server execution"]
    a7 -- "auto, sandbox, or gateway" --> a9["Runtime policy also checks discord-bot-a"]
    a9 --> a10["Attach, create, turn/start, and recovery use bound-agent policy"]
  end
```

## Proof
| Evidence | Claim Proved | How To Check |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Net diff: 8 Codex files, `+696/-31`, no branch-only churn | Current PR only changes binding data, bind command plumbing, guard/runtime policy input, native policy precedence, and focused tests | `git diff --stat 4e6057e4dd52abef405c20980edac4b3eb51a7ad...5e983a496d4a2b67527d6cf8eed9dda3a1c9268c` |
| Focused local Vitest red/green | Tests cover bind, bound-turn, recovery policy, node-host guard, current-session override masking, and default-session override preservation | `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-execution-policy.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` |
| Autoreview closeout | Final structured review found no accepted/actionable findings on `5e983a496d4a2b67527d6cf8eed9dda3a1c9268c` | `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --prompt-file /tmp/pr86360-autoreview-context.md` |
| Mermaid validation | Before/after flow renders through Mermaid CLI before posting | `mmdc -i /tmp/pr86360-final-flow.mmd -o /tmp/pr86360-final-flow.svg` |
| GitHub checks on `5e983a496d4a2b67527d6cf8eed9dda3a1c9268c` | Full PR validation restarted after the final push | `gh pr checks 86360 --repo openclaw/openclaw` |

## Verification
```text
python3 /Users/jmerhi/repos/AGENT.MDs/skills/pr-proof-pack/scripts/pr_net_diff.py --markdown --proof-plan
pass: 8 files changed, 696 insertions, 31 deletions; no branch-only churn

PATH=/Users/jmerhi/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v22.21.0/installation/bin:$PATH PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/Users/jmerhi/repos/claw-working-space/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-execution-policy.test.ts -- --reporter=dot
first run failed before production fix: expected node block, got gateway allow

PATH=/Users/jmerhi/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v22.21.0/installation/bin:$PATH PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/Users/jmerhi/repos/claw-working-space/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-execution-policy.test.ts -- --reporter=dot
pass: 1 file, 8 tests

PATH=/Users/jmerhi/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v22.21.0/installation/bin:$PATH PNPM_CONFIG_MODULES_DIR=/Users/jmerhi/repos/claw-working-space/openclaw/node_modules node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-execution-policy.test.ts -- --reporter=dot
pass: 3 files, 160 tests

git diff --check
pass
```
